channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month, NBC News reported. The trio were discovered deceased on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suns star Kevin Durant helped off court after injuring left leg
item.description: Phoenix Suns All-Star Kevin Durant was helped to the locker room with 6:57 left in the third quarter against Houston on Sunday night after falling to the floor in a collision with the Rockets' Jabari Smith Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their disagreements often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rachel Morin trial: Family 'confident' after mother killed on evening run
item.description: Randolph Rice, attorney for Rachel Morin’s family, joins NewsNation as pretrial proceedings begin Monday.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Figure skater who lost both parents in DC plane crash brings world championships crowd to its feet
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Maxim Naumov came to a stop in the middle of the ice, looked up at the sky and patted his heart. Then he mouthed a few words, in Russian, to his parents: “This is for you guys. You guys are with me. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump roars down multiple paths of retribution as he vowed. Some targets yield while others fight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The executive order directed at one of the country's most prestigious law firms followed a well-worn playbook as President Donald Trump roared down the road to retribution.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: New Mexico taps UC San Diego's Eric Olen to replace Richard Pitino
item.description: New Mexico hired UC San Diego's Eric Olen on Sunday as its next basketball coach. Olen will replace Richard Pitino, who left to become Xavier's coach on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: See a missing police officer post? It's likely part of a nationwide scam, authorities say
item.description: Have you seen a post on Facebook recently, asking you to be on the lookout for a missing police officer? Chances are, you're actually seeing a scam post, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Statham’s 'A Working Man’ upsets ‘Snow White’ to take No. 1 at the box office
item.description: In an unexpected upset, the Jason Statham thriller “A Working Man” has taken the No_ 1 spot at the box office, besting the rapidly declining performance of “Snow White.”.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Richard Chamberlain, TV actor who starred in 'Dr. Kildare,' dies at 90
item.description: Richard Chamberlain, the handsome hero of the 1960s television series “Dr. Kildare” who found a second career as “king of the miniseries,” has died at age 90.
item.dc:creator: Bob Thomas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar, killer of Selena, denied parole
item.description: The woman convicted of killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Perez has been denied parole after spending decades behind bars for fatally shooting the young singer at a Texas motel in 1995, the state’s parole board announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-North Dakota lawmaker gets 10 years for going to Europe with intent to pay for sex with a minor
item.description: A once-powerful former North Dakota lawmaker was sentenced Wednesday to 10 years in prison for traveling to Europe with the intent to pay for sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4
